title: SOG Encoding Pipeline: Quantize, Sort, Compress
description:
## Lab 5: Building the SOG Pipeline

This capstone lab implements the full Self-Organizing Gaussians encoding pipeline end-to-end: generate a synthetic 3DGS scene, quantize each attribute category, sort into a 2D grid, compress with image codecs, and decode — measuring compression ratio and reconstruction error at each stage.

### The Pipeline

```
Synthetic 3DGS data (N=65536 Gaussians, 59 params, float32)
    ↓ Quantization (per-attribute bit reduction)
Quantized integers (avg ~6-bit effective precision)
    ↓ Grid reshaping (N → √N × √N)
59 attribute images of size 256×256
    ↓ Morton-curve sorting (by Gaussian position)
Smooth 2D attribute images
    ↓ Image compression (PIL/Pillow JPEG at various qualities)
Compressed files
    ↓ Decode + dequantize
Reconstructed float attributes
```

### What You'll Measure

For each stage you will record the cumulative compressed size and compute the running compression ratio. The final section plots rate-distortion curves (PSNR vs. file size) for different JPEG quality settings, reproducing the key analysis from the SOG paper.
objectives: Implement per-attribute scalar quantization with appropriate bit depths for each 3DGS attribute category.
Reshape N Gaussians into a √N × √N grid and visualize the resulting attribute images.
Implement Morton (Z-order) curve sorting and measure the increase in local correlation.
Compress attribute grids with PIL JPEG at multiple quality levels and measure file sizes.
Implement the decode/dequantize pipeline and compute per-attribute RMSE.
Plot a rate-distortion curve and compare total compression ratio to the reported SOG ~22× result.
